one_liner: Ramp offers a broad OAuth2-secured REST Developer API for spend/finance data, plus separate MCP servers for documentation and account data access.

auth_methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code, Refresh Token, and Client Credentials grants. Token endpoint at https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token, authenticated via HTTP Basic (base64 client_id:client_secret), returns Bearer access token.

self_serve_notes: Requires a Ramp business account and app registration (client ID/secret) to obtain OAuth credentials; production API access may require admin permissions or a support/provisioning request. Sandbox available for testing without full production approval.

api_surface.notes: Ramp Developer API (v1) covers business financial data: transactions, cards, reimbursements, bills, users, etc. OpenAPI spec published at /openapi/developer-api.json.

main_blocker: Production API access requires a Ramp business account, OAuth app registration, and potentially admin approval/support ticket for provisioning; write operations are disabled in sandbox.

evidence.supports: OAuth2 flow details: client credentials, authorization code, refresh token grants; Basic auth for token endpoint; Bearer token usage.
Existence of Ramp MCP server(s) - both account-specific ramp-mcp and unauthenticated Developer MCP for docs.
Setup guide referenced for obtaining OAuth credentials and getting started with API.
Developer API access tied to business account plans, not necessarily free self-serve tier.

flags: MCP server confirmed to exist (two variants: ramp-mcp and Developer MCP)
Self-serve status ambiguous - requires business account and possibly admin approval for production scopes
Sandbox environment available but write operations disabled, limiting testing depth
